step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the components from the whiteboard and transcript. The main workflow consists of active canary monitoring and a status page for internal users. 1. Internal Stakeholders (UserCompanyInternalPlatform) access the frontend hosted on Amplify. 2. Amplify talks to AppSync for GraphQL APIs. 3. AppSync triggers a Lambda resolver. 4. Lambda queries CloudWatch for canary status and metadata. 5. CloudWatch Canaries are run on a schedule triggered by a Time Trigger (EventBridge) and save artifacts (screenshots, HAR files) to S3.

graph.name: HBO Outside-In Monitoring with CloudWatch Canaries
graph.notes: Serverless validation framework for monitoring HBO Max experiences globally. Uses CloudWatch Canaries deployed across multiple regions to simulate users, saving artifacts to S3 and exposing status via an Amplify/AppSync frontend.

nodes.name: Internal Stakeholders
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Incident response, customer care, and on-call teams viewing status.

nodes.name: Amplify
nodes.notes: Hosts the status page UI dashboard for stakeholders without AWS console access.

nodes.name: AppSync
nodes.notes: Provides a GraphQL interface to query canary status and alerts.

nodes.name: Lambda Resolver
nodes.notes: Uses AWS SDK to pull canary execution metadata, history, and screenshot logs.

nodes.name: CloudWatch
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Runs Synthetics Canaries every 1-5 minutes to test endpoints and manage alerting.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores canary test run artifacts, including HAR files and screenshots.

nodes.name: Time Trigger
nodes.notes: Schedules the canary tests to run automatically.

edges.notes: Triggers CloudWatch Synthetics Canaries on a schedule.
Saves HAR files and screenshots from canary runs.
Stakeholder accesses the experience status dashboard.
Sends GraphQL query for canary status.
Pulls historical runs, statuses, and logs.
